name: Take Profit Trader
description: Monthly-subscription evals with the fastest withdrawal rail in the field — uncapped, ~1-hour payouts once funded. PRO tier runs intraday trailing; PRO+ upgrade moves to 90/10 with EOD drawdown and no buffer.

name: Funded Futures Network
description: One-time-fee evals with the cleanest funded mechanics in the comparison: EOD trailing drawdown that locks static once the payout buffer is cleared. 2-day minimum pass, no daily loss limit.

name: TradeDay
description: Payout-velocity specialist. Quick Pay plans are built around fast withdrawals with no payout buffer, plus a trader's-choice drawdown model at signup.

name: Test 50K

promoPriceAndCode.description: 40% off for life + activation fee waived — ~$102/mo all-in.

fundedSplit.note: PRO: 80/20, intraday trailing, buffer required. PRO+ upgrade: 90/10, EOD drawdown, no buffer.

pathToLive: Test (eval) → PRO (sim-funded, intraday trailing) → PRO+ tier (90/10, EOD drawdown, no buffer).

Softest eval math with $1,500/day consistency cap
Caution: funded PRO stage flips to INTRADAY trailing drawdown
PRO reset $649 — hard 3-reset cap, then permanent account closure

pathToLive: Eval → sim-funded. EOD trailing drawdown locks STATIC (+$100) once the $2,000 payout buffer is cleared — the cleanest funded mechanics in this comparison.
keyHighlights: Strongest value: one-time $229 → $115 with MOT; BOGO ≈ $57.50/account
Fastest pass in the field: 2-day minimum
Funded drawdown goes STATIC after buffer — no intraday trailing anxiety
One-time fee: failed months don't compound like subscriptions
Friction: $52,000 payout buffer and 4-mini cap (scales 2→4 with profit)
VERIFY before publish: funded profit split, activation fee

title: The 50K Showdown — TPT Test vs FFN Steady vs TradeDay Quick Pay
excerpt: Three 50K evals with identical targets — and wildly different funded mechanics. TPT Test, FFN Steady, and TradeDay Quick Pay, decoded.
eyebrow: Sire Mammat's Lab · Prop Firm Intel · July 2026

content.heading: Same Eval, Different Endgame
content.body: All three plans run identical evaluation parameters: $3,000 target, $2,000 drawdown, no daily loss limit, and zero consistency requirements once funded. That makes the eval a wash — the real differentiator is what happens to your drawdown AFTER you pass. Only one of these plans lets the floor stop moving.

content.heading: The Evaluation, Side by Side
content.subheading: Columns: TPT Test · FFN Steady · TradeDay Quick Pay

content.heading: Funded Mechanics — Where the Field Separates
The Honest Caveats
content.body: TPT and TradeDay both flip you to intraday trailing drawdown the moment you're funded — your open P&L moves the floor in real time. FFN's friction is different: a $52,000 payout buffer before withdrawals and a 4-mini cap. None of these are dealbreakers; they're fit questions. Buy the mirror, not the dream.

verdict.heading: The Lab Verdict
paragraphs: With identical eval math across the board, the ranking comes down to economics and funded mechanics — and one plan wins both.

FFN Steady takes the crown: the cheapest effective entry in the field, a one-time fee that doesn't compound on failed months, the fastest pass, and the only funded drawdown here that stops trailing. TradeDay's payout velocity earns second. TPT's size and withdrawal speed keep it firmly in the conversation at third.

ranking.name: FFN Steady 50K
ranking.reason: Best value (≈$57.50/acct BOGO) + cleanest funded mechanics — drawdown locks static after buffer.

ranking.name: TradeDay Quick Pay 50K
ranking.reason: Payout velocity — fast withdrawals with no payout buffer.

ranking.name: TPT Test 50K
ranking.reason: Biggest size (6 minis) and uncapped ~1-hour withdrawals once funded.

disclaimers: Futures trading involves substantial risk of loss and is not suitable for all investors. Funded accounts are simulated trading accounts; payouts are made under each firm's published terms. Links may carry affiliate code MOT — the Lab may earn a commission at no cost to you. All specs verified against provider-published terms at time of writing; always confirm directly with providers before purchasing.
